This PR updates [pytest](https://pypi.org/project/pytest) from **3.7.0** to **3.7.2**.



<details>
  <summary>Changelog</summary>
  
  
   ### 3.7.2
   ```
   =========================

Bug Fixes
---------

- `3671 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3671>`_: Fix ``filterwarnings`` not being registered as a builtin mark.


- `3768 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3768>`_, `3789 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3789>`_: Fix test collection from packages mixed with normal directories.


- `3771 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3771>`_: Fix infinite recursion during collection if a ``pytest_ignore_collect`` hook returns ``False`` instead of ``None``.


- `3774 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3774>`_: Fix bug where decorated fixtures would lose functionality (for example ``mock.patch``).


- `3775 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3775>`_: Fix bug where importing modules or other objects with prefix ``pytest_`` prefix would raise a ``PluginValidationError``.


- `3788 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3788>`_: Fix ``AttributeError`` during teardown of ``TestCase`` subclasses which raise an exception during ``__init__``.


- `3804 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3804>`_: Fix traceback reporting for exceptions with ``__cause__`` cycles.



Improved Documentation
----------------------

- `3746 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3746>`_: Add documentation for ``metafunc.config`` that had been mistakenly hidden.
   ```
   
  
  
   ### 3.7.1
   ```
   =========================

Bug Fixes
---------

- `3473 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3473>`_: Raise immediately if ``approx()`` is given an expected value of a type it doesn't understand (e.g. strings, nested dicts, etc.).


- `3712 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3712>`_: Correctly represent the dimensions of an numpy array when calling ``repr()`` on ``approx()``.

- `3742 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3742>`_: Fix incompatibility with third party plugins during collection, which produced the error ``object has no attribute '_collectfile'``.

- `3745 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3745>`_: Display the absolute path if ``cache_dir`` is not relative to the ``rootdir`` instead of failing.


- `3747 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3747>`_: Fix compatibility problem with plugins and the warning code issued by fixture functions when they are called directly.


- `3748 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3748>`_: Fix infinite recursion in ``pytest.approx`` with arrays in ``numpy<1.13``.


- `3757 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3757>`_: Pin pathlib2 to ``>=2.2.0`` as we require ``__fspath__`` support.


- `3763 <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3763>`_: Fix ``TypeError`` when the assertion message is ``bytes`` in python 3.
   ```

pr_title: INDY-1497: Fix sending to not connected remotes
pr_body:
**Problem reason:**

- If a message is sent to a node which hasn't yet replied with pong (that is connected), then there is a chance that the message will be lost and not re-sent. This may lead to losing messages, especially during initial catch-up.

**Fix:**

- Stash messages sent to disconnected node and re-send them once we got both ping and pong from them (that is both nodes are being connected and known to each other).
- The queue for stashing is limited to avoid out of memory issues.
- Stash and re-send pongs to not yet connected entities.
Signed-off-by: Andrew Nikitin <andrew.nikitin@dsr-corporation.com>

pr_title: Don't rely on 'python' in path during testing
pr_body:
The test_python test relies on finding an executable called "python" in the path. The symlink from python to python2 is not guaranteed to exist. And when it is not present the test fails as follows:
````
test_python (metakernel.tests.test_replwrap.REPLWrapTestCase) ... ERROR
======================================================================
ERROR: test_python (metakernel.tests.test_replwrap.REPLWrapTestCase)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/builddir/build/BUILD/metakernel-0.20.14/metakernel/tests/test_replwrap.py", line 62, in test_python
    p = replwrap.python()
  File "/builddir/build/BUILD/metakernel-0.20.14/metakernel/replwrap.py", line 214, in python
    u("import sys; sys.ps1={0!r}; sys.ps2={1!r}"))
  File "/builddir/build/BUILD/metakernel-0.20.14/metakernel/replwrap.py", line 66, in __init__
    encoding="utf-8")
  File "/builddir/build/BUILD/metakernel-0.20.14/metakernel/pexpect.py", line 49, in spawn
    encoding=encoding, codec_errors=codec_errors)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pexpect/pty_spawn.py", line 204, in __init__
    self._spawn(command, args, preexec_fn, dimensions)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pexpect/pty_spawn.py", line 276, in _spawn
    'executable: %s.' % self.command)
ExceptionPexpect: The command was not found or was not executable: python.
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 77 tests in 33.071s
FAILED (errors=1)
````
This PR changes the test to explicitly request sys.python (i.e. the python version used to run the test - and therefore know to be present) instead of relying on the default value "python". With this change the test succeeds also when the python symlink is not in the path.

pr_title: Added optional directory display and image grouping to the image browser
pr_body:
While implementing the module into my site I figured it would be handy if the image browser displayed directories, as well as grouped the images so they don't show up as a big wall of thumbnails with no way of figuring out when they were uploaded and/or used. So I've developed this small feature - if enabled, the image browser now displays directories and thumbnails of images that belong to them, sorted by date.

Enabling the grouping requires setting the ``CKEDITOR_BROWSE_SHOW_DIRS`` to ``True``. My code iterates through the ``files`` list of dictionaries right before passing it to the template, and creates a set of unique directories out of images present in ``files``, then sorts it using the built-in ``sorted()`` function. 

``dir_list = sorted(set(os.path.dirname(f['src']) for f in files), reverse=True)``

Context contains two additional elements: ``show_dirs``, a boolean taken from the ``CKEDITOR_BROWSE_SHOW_DIRS`` setting, as well as ``dirs``, a sorted list of directories.

The ``browse.html`` template changes are self-explanatory. For every directory, display the directory path and iterate through files checking if the path matches, which essentially results in images getting grouped by directory they're in. Commit 866c242 shows a previous implementation that I've decided to change. It took less lines, however with a downside: code executed even if the feature was disabled, which basically means you couldn't truly disable the feature. I've decided to put the ``{% if show_dirs %}`` statement first and duplicate the contents of ``<ul class="thumbs noscript">`` - more duplicated lines and sort of less readable at first glance, but easier to understand what is going on, and actually works as intended.

There is an additional fix in the view, for removing Thumbs.db files from the list, an issue I ran across while testing. I guess it could stay, given it's enclosed in conditional statement and will only run on Windows. If there's a better place for that, please let me know.

I've also updated readme to take note of the new feature.

pr_title: Remove the old JobRepository and JobRepositoryXML directories
pr_body: As far as I can tell, these are completely superseded by `python/Ganga/Core/GangaRepository` and are not referenced anywhere in the code any more. As discussed in the last meeting, this code was confusing us when trying to debug repository problems so it should be removed.

This PR addresses several different things.
Fixes #786 

 1. This class fixes a bug in the `Ganga/Utility/execute.py` a deepcopy is needed to avoid a random difficult to track down bug
 2. I've added `GangDiracException` which is thrown from `GangaDirac/Lib/Utility/DiracUtilities.py`
 3. I've added a wrapper within the DIRAC server code which ensures that a dictionary object is always returned through the streams to Ganga.
 4. By default I've made sure that the ~~`DiracUtility`~~ `execute` function (`GangaDirac/Lib/Utilities/DiracUtilities.py`) now returns the `Value` or raises an appropriate `GangaDiracException` as this is the safest and imo best default implementation.
We can change this to return the dict by default and only inspect the dict on request but this is a point for discussion.
 5. I've separated tests which require a DIRAC proxy from tests which don't require a DIRAC proxy.
All tests requiring something to be returned via the stdout require a proxy otherwise DIRAC pollutes the stdout. If the test wants to test the executable method only it should do so explicitly.
This is not exposed to users and users are required to have a DIRAC proxy to talk to DIRAC within Ganga.
 6. Updated the tests to check for the dict always being returned from the `execute` method. This allows for better test failures.
 7. Updated the appropriate code in `GangaLHCb`, `DiracBase` and `DiracFile` to work correctly with these changes.

I'm not looking to merge this now but the tests should work.
If they don't I've made a mistake in rebasing this and will do so again later today.

Feel free to take a look through the code and as always.

Edit:
Also:
 1. Migrated the execute tests to Core tests. Fixed some bugs exposed by using this over the Dirac wrapper to this.

- Moved all of the git-related method from `catalog.catalog.Catalog` into (new) `catalog.gitter` submodule.
- rewrote the `gitter.git_pull_all_repos()` command to pull all repos of the passed catalog.

Waiting for @guillochon to test before merging.

The current setup is to run `git pull -s recursive -X theirs` by default, but both options correspond to boolean arguments to the function.  If `git pull` returns a non-zero exit status, the error is logged and an error is raised.  For example, if there are local changes to the files, it will complain that they need to be committed or stashed to avoid overwriting... and an error will be raised.  In this case, the user could either run the `git-push` subcommand to commit and push the changes, or toss the local changes using `git-reset-origin`.  Using `--ff-only` to only accept fast-forward changes might be something to think about for the future.

Then google-cloud-java team can easily write a 20-line python script (excluding constant string list pointing to different artman yaml) to do batch refresh. And any customization, if necessary, can be done in the repo-specific batch script without coupling with artman.

pr_title: Support quoted server names in Nginx
pr_body:
Fixes #5543.

Here's the thing. This is a perfectly fine way to solve this problem.

A slightly more complete solution would go and check that we're removing quotes everywhere else they might be. This is clearly the wrong choice, we can wait until we get complaints about other places to go fix those.

A more complete way to solve this problem is to revamp the Nginx parsing infrastructure, so we can do all the things you're supposed to do at parse time like removing quotes and whitespace. Then we can kill unspaced list! And then we'd need a different solution for writing things back out that handles detecting formatting and where to put things. There's multiple perfectly good ways to handle that, which I'm not writing out here because they're pretty obvious. This would also solve at least one other open parsing-related ticket regarding strings and quotes.

The _best_ way to solve this tiny problem is by rewriting and packaging certbot as just a cert-management library that a plugin to the Nginx server could pull in. This is because the authoritative parsing is what Nginx does, and also because it's so much better of a UX.

In the meantime, here's a 25-character fix.

pr_title: Suggested modification to improve reuse as library
pr_body:
### Proposal: Enable and simplify code reuse by providing an output plugin that supppresses gratuitous output

Let us assume I just need the provided OTC API related code because I do not want to track, maintain and duplicate any REST API calls, e.g. I was intending to write ansible modules in the style of already cloud modules like here: http://docs.ansible.com/ansible/list_of_cloud_modules.html

Unfortunately all provided output modules mess up my plans because ansible reads STDOUT and STDERR from module output and currently both do not emit anything helpful for this usecase.

This PR does two things:

1. Allow us to have a configurable settle timeout less than the default one. For some reason the API call was taking the default as the minimum.
2. If a channel has already been opened in the blockchain with invalid parameters we can recover from the error and simply ignore it.

As of python3.3, the recommended method for obtaining a performance
timer is no longer "time.clock()" but "time.perf_counter()".  By using
time.perf_counter, we outsource the problem for finding the best
performance messaurement to python.  Plus, we also avoid inaccurate
timings if the system time is changed during the processing of the SVG
image on platforms where we used to use "time.time()".

For python2 and python3 before 3.3, we still have to use the original
timers.

Signed-off-by: Niels Thykier <niels@thykier.net>

pr_title: [Issues 2403][xray] Fix raylet performance issues on scheduling queue
pr_body:
NOTE:    As mentioned in the issue thread, there are some other PRs related to scheduling queue, current plan is to wait for these PRs to merge first, and revisit this PR. 

## What do these changes do?

During performance testing, I found the latest raylet has significant performance regressions when I tried to transfer 100K integers from one actor to another using a python script, on my local macbook. The script previously takes 40s but right now cannot finish in 15+ minutes.

With the fix, running the same script on raylet gets back down to about 40s again.

## Related issue number

#2403
